brief_title: A Comparison Between BMS-690514 and Erlotinib in Patients Who Were Previously Treated for NSCLC
official_title: A Double-Blind, Randomized, Parallel Two-Arm Phase II Trial of BMS-690514 Versus Erlotinib in Previously Treated NSCLC Patients
brief_summary: The purpose of this study is to improve disease control and survival for patients who were treated with chemotherapy using BMS-690514 over erlotinib

interventions.name: BMS-690514
interventions.description: Tablets, Oral, 200 mg, once daily, Until disease progression or toxicity

interventions.name: Erlotinib
interventions.description: Capsules, Oral, 150 mg, once daily, Until disease progression or toxicity

eligibility.criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* ECOG PS of 0 or 1
* Histologically confirmed NSCLC
* Adequate amount of tumor (archived or fresh) for biomarker evaluation
* Received one to two regimens of chemotherapy (with at least one platinum-containing)
* Serum creatinine of less than 1.0 mg/dL or a 24 hour creatinine clearance of greater than 60 mL/min
* Stable control of blood pressure on agents other than calcium channel blockers
* Women of child-bearing potential must avoid pregnancy or maintain adequate contraception
* Must be able to swallow pills and take the medications at the same time every day on an empty stomach

Exclusion Criteria:

* ECOG PS 2 or greater
* Women unwilling to avoid pregnancy or use adequate contraception
* Symptomatic brain metastases
* Recent history of TIA, CVA, or thrombotic/thromboembolic event (within 6 months)
* History of hemoptysis greater than 10 mL/day
* Significant cardiovascular disease
* Uncontrolled diarrhea, Crohn's disease, ulcerative colitis, or any malabsorptive disease
* History of use of other TKIs
* Uncontrolled hypertension
* HIV+

primary_outcomes.measure: To compare the progression-free survival of patients on BMS-690514 with those on erlotinib
primary_outcomes.timeFrame: CT/MRI at baseline and every 6 weeks for 36 weeks
